papers.title: Current state of Fe-Mn-Al-C low density steels
papers.paper_scope: Critical review of physical metallurgy, processing strategies, strengthening mechanisms and mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C low density steels for automotive structural applications, covering ferritic, duplex and austenitic alloy classes.

alloys.alloys_notes: Fully austenitic lightweight steel exhibiting microband induced plasticity (MBIP).
Austenitic steel studied for dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR).
Austenite based duplex steel (triplex) exhibiting shear band induced plasticity (SIP).
Age hardenable austenitic steel with kappa-prime precipitation.
Austenitic steel used for aging and impact toughness studies.
Austenitic steel used for impact toughness comparisons.
Austenitic steel used for tensile and impact toughness studies.

Austenite based duplex steel used for formability assessment.
Austenite based duplex steel used for formability assessment.

processes.description: Solution treated at 1000°C for 60 min and water quenched; produces single austenite phase with SRO.
Solution treated at 1100°C for 120 min and oil quenched; produces austenite with SRO.
Solution treated at 1050°C for 25 min and water quenched; produces austenite plus ~8% delta-ferrite and nano-sized kappa-prime carbides.
Aged at 550°C for approximately 10 h after solution treatment; produces peak hardness via kappa-prime precipitation.
processes.processes_notes: Age hardening response studied for peak hardness.

processes.description: Water quenched from 1050°C and aged at 550°C for 16 h.
processes.processes_notes: Aged condition for tensile and impact property studies.

processes.description: Solution treated at 1050°C for 75 min and water quenched.
processes.processes_notes: Solution treated condition for impact toughness testing.

processes.description: Solution treated at 1050°C for 1 h and water quenched.
Cold rolled to 70% thickness reduction and annealed at 900°C for 2 min.
processes.processes_notes: Thermomechanical processing to produce austenite plus B2 stringer bands and nano-B2 precipitates.

processes.description: Cold rolled and recrystallization annealed at 700-950°C.
processes.processes_notes: Standard processing for ferritic Fe-Al steels to control grain size and texture.

processes.description: Annealed at 900°C for 30 min and water quenched; produces bimodal structure.
processes.processes_notes: Intercritical annealing to obtain TRIP/TWIP effects from retained austenite.

Annealed at 850°C for 3 min and fast cooled.

processes.processes_notes: Water quenched condition showing negative work hardening rate.

structures.overall_structure: Single phase austenite with short-range order (SRO)
Single phase austenite with short-range order
Duplex austenite plus delta-ferrite with intragranular kappa-prime carbides

precipitates.distribution: along grain boundaries and shear bands

structures.overall_structure: Bimodal ferrite matrix with clustered austenite particles

defect_interaction.quality_description: Dislocations pile up or bow out at alpha(B2) phase boundaries; B2 particles are not sheared by gliding dislocations

characterization_methods.key_findings: Detected austenite and ferrite; kappa-prime not detected by XRD
Pronounced planar slip and crystallographically aligned slip bands; dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR) observed
SRO zones <2 nm observed in austenite after solution treatment and quenching
B2 stringer bands and nano-sized B2 particles along austenite grain boundaries and shear bands
Recrystallized delta-ferrite grains ranging from 40 to 90 μm
Peak hardness ~340 HB after aging at 550°C for approximately 10 h

computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Calculated phase diagrams compared with experimental isotherms and polythermal sections; reasonable agreement at moderate compositions but deviations noted at high Mn and high Al contents

unmapped_findings: Specific stiffness (E^n/ρ) of low density steels is not higher than HSLA steels and aluminium alloys; weight saving requires application in parts where stiffness is not the limiting factor.
Young's modulus of cold rolled Fe-20Mn-(7-10)Al-(0.7-1.2)C steels is approximately 20% lower than HSLA grades (~210 GPa).
Stacking fault energy values reported: Fe-28Mn-9Al-0.8C ~85 mJ/m^2; Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C ~120 mJ/m^2; Fe-30.4Mn-8Al-1.2C ~85 mJ/m^2; Fe-27Mn-12Al-0.9C ~110 mJ/m^2; Fe-18Mn-9.6Al-0.65C ~66 mJ/m^2.
Solution-treated austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C steels exhibit CVN impact energy of ~200 J/cm^2 at room temperature, comparable to austenitic stainless steels; aging at 550°C greatly reduces toughness.
Fe-30Mn-10Al-1C-1Si in solution-treated condition shows CVN ~200 J/cm^2 at RT; after aging at 550°C for 16 h impact energy is reduced to almost zero.
Fe-28Mn-5Al-1C solutionized at 1050°C for 75 min shows CVN impact energy ~200 J/cm^2 at RT.
Dynamic fracture toughness (J-integral) of solution-treated Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.9C-xSi is ~71.5 J/cm^2, much higher than age-hardened conditions (9.5-37.5 J/cm^2).
Specific energy absorption of Fe-Mn-Al-C steels with Al>7% is ~0.5 J/mm^3, similar to Mn TWIP steels and about 50% higher than conventional deep drawing steels.
Corrosion resistance of Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys is inferior to 304 stainless steel and comparable to conventional high strength steels.
Fatigue behaviour data are conflicting and insufficient for generalised conclusions; some reports show cyclic softening associated with planar slip and shearing of kappa-carbides.
Formability data are limited; hole expansion ratio of austenite based duplex steels is comparable to DP600 but bending angle and dome expansion are lower than conventional AHSS.
Precipitation of intragranular kappa-prime carbide in austenitic steels requires Al >7% and C >0.7%.
Glide plane softening due to short-range order or kappa-prime carbides leads to planar slip regardless of high stacking fault energy.
Deformation mechanisms shift from TRIP/TWIP to MBIP, DSBR, and SIP as Al and C contents increase.
Negative work hardening rate is observed in highly alloyed Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.5C and peak-aged Fe-28Mn-12Al-1C, which existing mechanisms cannot fully explain.
Industrial processing challenges include Al2O3 nozzle clogging, surface oxide formation, cracking during hot and cold forming, and reduced solidus temperatures due to segregation.
